steps.text: Submit a rule report to each House of the Congress and to the Comptroller General of the United States.

preconditions.text: This action is subject to the Congressional Review Act (CRA).

steps.text: Withdraw gaseous and particulate bound target pollutants from the gas stream isokinetically and collect them in the sample probe, on a glass fiber or quartz filter, and on a packed column of adsorbent material.
Extract the target compounds from the combined sample collection media.
Chromatographically fractionate portions of the extract to remove interferences.
Separate the fractionated extract into individual compounds or simple mixtures by HRGC.
Measure the separated compounds with HRMS.
Measure the gas temperature at the entrance to the adsorbent module.
Maintain this temperature at <20 °C (68 °F).
Orient the adsorbent module vertically to facilitate condensate drainage.
Include a coarse glass frit in the adsorbent module to retain the adsorbent.
Connect five impingers in series with leak-free ground glass fittings or any similar leak-free noncontaminating fittings.
Purchase precleaned amber glass jars and petri dishes, or clean them according to the glassware cleaning procedures listed in Section 8.1.1.1.
Clean heavy duty aluminum foil by rinsing three times with hexane or toluene.
Store the cleaned aluminum foil in a pre-cleaned glass petri dish or glass jar.

preconditions.text: If you do not expect excess moisture in the sample gas

constraints.text: Do not use aluminum foil to wrap or contact filter samples due to the possibility of reaction between the sample and the aluminum.
The connecting fittings must form leak-free, vacuum-tight seals.

steps.text: Calibrate and standardize the Sampling System according to Sections 6.1 and 10.1 through 10.7 of Method 5 of Appendix A–3 to 40 CFR part 60.
Tune the HRMS instrument to a resolving power of at least 10,000 at 10% of the peak height or 25,000 at 50% of the peak height.
Assess the resolution at three exact m/z’s representing the low-, mid-, and high-m/z range of the masses used to measure the target compound class.
Verify that the exact mass is within 5 ppm of the required value, optionally using peak matching and the chosen perfluoro-kerosene (PFK) or perfluorotributylamine (FC43) reference peak.
Calibrate the HRGC/HRMS system using a minimum of five concentrations over a range that brackets expected field sample concentrations and the concentration of isotopically labeled

preconditions.text: For PAH and PCB analysis, the resolving power may be 8,000 at 10% of the peak height or 15,000 at 50% of the peak height.

steps.text: Concentrate initial extracts in toluene using a heating mantle and three-ball Snyder column or a rotary evaporator.
Concentrate the field train proof blank and LMB samples in the same manner as samples.
Allow the sample extract to cool.
Use a minimum of one half of the sample extract for PCDD/PCDF analysis.
Archive the remaining sample extract or further split the sample extract for PCB and/or PAH analysis and archive.
If necessary, further concentrate the sample extract for cleanup and analysis using concentration techniques found to recover the pre-extraction standard.
Process a separate aliquot/split of the sample extract for each of the compound classes analyzed by this method.
Evaluate the cleanup and fractionation procedures used to confirm acceptable recovery of isotopically labeled standards.

preconditions.text: If using an amount other than half the sample extract, adjust the spiking amount of the labeled standards accordingly.

constraints.text: To meet isotopically labeled standard recoveries for low molecular weight PCB and PAH, do not evaporate samples to dryness and do not use a rotary evaporator to concentrate extracts.

steps.text: Flag data from this analysis explaining the potential bias of the results.
Analyze sample (or another aliquot of the sample) using an alternative column that provides elution order to uniquely quantify the target compounds subject to interference on the first GC column.
Document the required resolution.
Determine MDLs following the procedures in Section 9.3.7 of this method.

preconditions.text: Failure to meet chromatographic resolution criterion.

If target compounds are not sufficiently resolved from other target compounds or interferences in the sample to meet the requirements for target compounds in Sections 13.2, 13.3, and/or 13.4 of this method.

steps.text: Confirm that the LMB is free from contamination as defined in Section 13.1.
Select the labeled standard having the same number of chlorine substituents and the closest retention time to the target analyte in question as the labeled standard to use for quantitation.

preconditions.text: In the event you choose another column other than the SPB-Octyl column.

steps.text: Place resin in a suitable container, soak for approximately 5 min with Type II water, remove fine floating resin particles and discard the water.
Fill with Type II water a second time, let stand overnight, remove fine floating resin particles, and discard the water.
Extract with hot water for 8 hr.
Extract with methyl alcohol for 22 hr.
Extract with methylene chloride for 22 hr.
Extract with toluene for 22 hr.
Extract with methylene chloride for 22 hr.

preconditions.text: You may store the resin in a sealed glass container filled with toluene prior to the final toluene extraction.
It may be necessary to repeat the final methylene chloride extractions to meet the cleanliness requirements in Section 13.1 of Method 23.

constraints.text: Because the resin floats on methylene chloride, carefully retain the resin in the extractor cup with a glass wool plug and stainless-steel screen.
All materials of construction are glass, PTFE, or stainless steel. Pumps, if used, should not contain extractable materials.
Use an all-glass thimble containing an extra-coarse frit for extraction of the resin.
